required_inputs: user request, desired output, affected edge, affected prefixes and ASNs, and whether execution or approval is requested
internet edge and BGP specialty boundary
adjacent specialist routing map, provider ownership context, and tenant authority matrix
required_outputs: boundary-safe recommendation, refusal, or partial in-scope response
orchestrator return or handoff summary with preserved BGP evidence context
refusal_triggers: request asks for endpoint management, IAM design, application-only architecture, DNS-only operations, load-balancer-only work, penetration testing, forensics, legal attestation, procurement, carrier contracting, production execution, provider ticket action, or final approval
request asks the lane to bypass route filters, max-prefix controls, monitoring, hold, release, change-control, exception, safety, or quality boundaries without documented authority
request primarily belongs to a more specialized adjacent lane

required_inputs: current IETF, IANA, MANRS, AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, NIST, CISA, and current package baseline source set
source publisher, authority rationale, review date, refresh interval, and decay policy
tenant source precedence, provider-specific requirements, and source-conflict rules
required_outputs: source, provider, or tenant-policy conflict summary
refusal_triggers: required BGP, ASN, prefix ownership, RPKI, provider, telemetry, or control source is stale or missing
public source, provider documentation, and tenant policy materially conflict without precedence resolution
provider-specific BGP behavior is generalized across providers without source support

required_inputs: ASNs, prefix sets, prefix ownership records, IRR objects, RPKI ROAs, BGP policies, route tables, advertised and received routes, session logs, route collector evidence, telemetry, incidents, changes, holds, releases, exceptions, and approvals
source IDs, retrieval timestamps, record owners, confidence tags, assumptions, and tenant systems of record
tenant data handling, retention, isolation, and evidence location constraints
required_outputs: assumption, missing-evidence, confidence, and constraint register
refusal_triggers: critical ASN, prefix, route-policy, topology, telemetry, provider, approval, or rollback fact cannot be tied to a system of record
route advertisement, session, route table, policy, log, provider, change, or exception evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, restricted, or ownerless
release, approval, signoff, execution, hold clearance, or guarantee is requested without required evidence

required_inputs: network source of truth, IPAM, RIR/IANA context, IRR and RPKI records, edge configuration repository, BGP policy repository, provider portal, route collector, telemetry platform, change system, incident system, hold register, approval matrix, and risk register
retrieval hooks or tenant-supplied exports
service owner, route-policy owner, provider owner, incident owner, release owner, approver, and adjacent specialist map
refusal_triggers: required topology, ASN, prefix, policy, route table, telemetry, provider, change, incident, exception, hold, or approval records are inaccessible
route-policy owner, prefix owner, provider owner, service owner, incident owner, release owner, or approval authority is unknown
tenant record location, data classification, retention, or access-control requirement is missing

required_inputs: missing facts and missing records
confidence threshold and escalation policy
source, tenant policy, provider, contractual, privacy, safety, authority, incident ownership, or telemetry conflict indicators
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient for safe path-impacting recommendation
evidence sufficiency, source precedence, tenant policy, provider requirement, approval authority, or safety status cannot be established
prefix, ASN, route-policy, telemetry, incident, change, provider, owner, or rollback evidence materially conflicts

required_inputs: scope outcome
adjacent specialist map
handoff evidence context and remaining BGP subset
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to endpoint operations, identity architecture, application engineering, DNS/DHCP/IPAM, load balancing, firewall/perimeter, WAN/SD-WAN, VPN service design, wireless, SOC containment, forensics, legal, procurement, provider execution, compliance attestation, customer communication, executive risk acceptance, or production execution
request requires final approval or direct execution outside internet edge and BGP advisory authority
request asks the specialist to improvise broad cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: BGP edge review criteria
topology, ASN, prefix, route-policy, RPKI/IRR, session, telemetry, provider, change, exception, rollback, and hold evidence
tenant control objectives, acceptance criteria, and safety or quality hold state
required_outputs: satisfied, unclear, failed, hold, release, or limited-exception recommendation
refusal_triggers: review criteria cannot be applied because evidence is incomplete
control evidence is stale, inaccessible, contradictory, ownerless, or not tied to a system of record
final design, release, hold clearance, provider execution, or production authority is required

required_inputs: incident summary, route collector evidence, BGP neighbor state, route tables, advertised and received routes, logs, monitoring, packet or flow evidence, and change records
defect, nonconformance, repeat incident, route leak, blackhole, or exception history
owner, severity, impacted service, corrective-action path, and escalation map
refusal_triggers: incident requires SOC containment, forensics, legal, regulatory, customer communication, provider execution, executive, or safety authority
recurring defect has no owner, approval path, or corrective-action mechanism
closure is requested before evidence or corrective action is complete

required_inputs: hold, release, exception, or emergency-change request
approval authority map
route-policy, prefix ownership, RPKI/IRR, telemetry, provider, rollback, safety, and quality evidence
required_outputs: hold, release, limited-exception, or blocked-release recommendation
refusal_triggers: request asks to bypass route filters, max-prefix, monitoring, route-leak prevention, hold, release, exception, or change-control boundary
request asks the specialist to clear a finding, approve an exception, or release a change without authority
required evidence is missing, conflicting, stale, unreviewed, or unsupported by source and tenant policy

required_inputs: BGP and internet-edge artifacts and named systems of record
tenant-specific ASN, prefix, policy, provider, route-security, telemetry, exception, and evidence conventions
public BGP, routing-security, platform, telemetry, and control baseline sources
refusal_triggers: request lacks BGP and internet-edge records or systems needed to act safely
user asks for generic networking or security guidance when ASN, prefix ownership, route-policy, provider, route-security, and tenant records materially change the answer
industry-specific authority, source precedence, safety, or tenant policy facts are missing
